name: airspace-mapper
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``airspace-mapper`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.
dependencies: python3 (>=3.8, stdlib only: sys, os, json, csv, xml.etree.ElementTree, datetime, dataclasses, typing)
input artifacts: .22000 handshake file (from hcxtools/hcxdumptool), .tsv signal log, .gpx GPS track (Kismet/gpsd)
algorithms: .22000 WPA*02* hash-line parser: splits on '*', extracts AP MAC (parts[3]) and hex-decoded SSID (parts[5]), dedups by ssid_mac key
OUI vendor lookup: first-6-hex MAC prefix matched against oui.csv, vendor-name normalization with 'Unknown' fallback
strongest-signal-per-MAC correlation from TSV (timestamp\tsignal\mac), default -100 dBm
GPS timestamp correlation: linear nearest-fix search over sorted GPX trkpt/wpt, accepts match only within +/-300s (5 min) of earliest TSV timestamp per MAC
de-duplication + metadata normalization across hash/signal/GPS/vendor sources
GPX waypoint export with SSID name, MAC/vendor/signal description
produces: airspace_analysis_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json (per-target: ssid, ap_mac, vendor, signal dBm, hash_line, lat/lon)
waypoints_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.gpx (geospatial waypoints for mapping tools, optional via --export-gpx)
console summary with target counts and output filenames
novelty: Not an airodump-ng wrapper — it does no sniffing at all. It is a pure offline fusion/enrichment layer over artifacts KFIOSA already produces: it joins hcxtools .22000 hashes with a signal TSV and a GPX track and emits one vendor-enriched, signal-prioritized, GPS-located triage JSON. airodump-ng+gpsd can GPS-tag live captures but does not correlate already-captured handshake hashes with per-MAC strongest-signal and OUI vendor into a single cracking-priority dataset; hcxtools emits raw hashes without geo/vendor context. The GPS-by-±5min-timestamp nearest-fix matcher and the signal-priority triage view are the genuinely new bits. Modest scope (2 files, stdlib-only) and the algorithms are simple string/tsv/xml parsing, but the composition is a real (if small) enrichment stage rather than a shell-out to existing tools.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
